rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH SPISAK VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2024 in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is HEARING REQUIRED. No opposition filed. Plaintiff's proposed First Amended Complaint adds five additional plaintiffs to the action. Plaintiff's moving papers do not request leave to add these legal heirs as plaintiffs; nor does the proposed order contain any language ordering the addition of additional plaintiffs. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 30, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SIGG ET AL VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2024 in Department 301, Line 3. Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed. 1. The trial date is August 19, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is August 27, 2024. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 13, 2024. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is August 2, 2024. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is August 16, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 30, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than May 13, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. 9. A further case management conference is set for June 25, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. a. Case management conference statements are not required. b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom. c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing. 10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than July 8, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due July 22, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on July 29, 2024. 11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, August 15, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. (Tentative ruling continued, see next entry)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY DEASY VS. FERGUSON ENTERPRISES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2024 in Department 301, Line 5. Defendant Westburne Supply, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to Exhibits H and I in the Woods Declaration (relevant portions of the 1994 and 2023 Deposition Transcripts of Anthony Piazza) is sustained. Even if the testimony was considered, the Court does not find that Piazza's former testimony flatly contradicts Plaintiff's response to Special Interrogatory No. 1 enough to shift the burden. The evidence is not what Defendant purports it to be. Piazza's failure in 1994 to recall the names of suppliers of "asbestos containing products" does not establish that he lacks or lacked information regarding "products" D supplied to his job sites. With respect to the 2023 former testimony, Defendant failed to provide the Court with the question preceding the answer submitted as evidence. Accordingly, the Court can infer nothing from this evidence. Defendant fails to sustain its initial burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that Plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to Defendant. (See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant chose not to depose Plaintiff's product-identification witness, Anthony Piazza. Defendant failed to address documents identified in Plaintiff's interrogatory responses naming various deposition testimonies in other cases or persons with knowledge identified in Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses, including Kevin Krausgrill, and Stewart Escalle. (Wood Decl., Exh. E [Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Special Interrogatories] at 4, 6, 9, 10.) Defendant fails to foreclose the possibility that Plaintiffs may yet prove asbestos exposure and causation attributed to Defendant. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 30, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: BARNEY LAWRENCE ET AL VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set A Preferential Trial Date Pursuant To C.C.P. 36(A)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 30, 2024 in Department 301, Line 6. Plaintiff's Motion To Set A Preferential Trial Date Pursuant to CCP 36(a) is OFF CALENDAR. (1) No POS of summons and complaint or Answer on file for: (i) General Electric Company; (ii) Crane Corporation; (iii) IMO Industries; (iv) Shell Oil; (v) Paramount Global; and (vi) Union Carbide Corporation. While a party may support their motion for preference with a declaration stating that all essential parties have been served with process or have appeared, (C.C.P. 36(c)(1)), any such declaration is undermined where, as here, there is no corresponding proof of service or answer on file. (2) No proper Proof of Service on file. (CRC rule 3.1300(c).) The Court is unable to determine whether all parties were properly served with the instant motion. Plaintiff's POS does not contain a list of the recipients served and instead refers the court to a "Transaction Receipt located on the File & Serve Xpress website." The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on April 30, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)
